title: Nvidia Q4 FY26 Earnings Call | $NVDA | 🔴 WATCH LIVE
transcript: Heat. Hey, Heat. Heat. Heat. N. Heat. Heat. >> [music] >> Heat. Heat. Happy birthday. Heat. Heat. [music] Heat. Heat. By daddy gy. [music] I'm doo. Heat. Heat. >> [music] [music] >> Hey everybody. Heat. Heat. Little heat. >> [music] >> Heat. Hey, Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Good afternoon. My name is Sarah and I will be your conference operator today. At this time, I would like to welcome everyone to Nvidia's fourth quarter earnings call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent any background noise. After the speaker's remarks, there will be a question and answer session. If you would like to ask a question during this time, simply press star followed by the number one on your telephone keypad. If you would like to withdraw your question, press star one again. Thank you. So, you may begin your conference. >> Thank you. Good afternoon everyone and welcome to Nvidia's conference call for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026. With me today from Nvidia are Jensen Wong, president and chief executive officer, and Colette Crest, executive vice president and chief financial officer. Our call is being webcast live on Nvidia's investor relations website. The webcast will be available for replay until the conference call to discuss our financial results for the first quarter of fiscal 2027. The content of today's call is Nvidia's property. It can't be reproduced or transcribed without our prior written consent. During this call, we may make forward-looking statements based on current expectations. These are subject to a number of significant risks and uncertainties, and our actual results may differ materially. For a discussion of factors that could affect our future financial results in business, please refer to the disclosure in today's earnings release, our most recent forms 10K and 10Q, and the reports that we may file on form 8K with the Securities and Exchange Commission. All our statements are m made as as of today, February 25th, 2026 based on informat

earnings_release.guidance_text: Q1 FY27 매출 가이던스 $430억 (±2%), 컨센서스 $420억 상회

minute_summaries.summary: CFO 콜레트 크레스: 어젠틱 AI의 ChatGPT 모먼트 도래 선언, Anthropic·Meta·OpenAI·xAI 파트너십 전방위 확대
Groq 저지연 추론 기술 비독점 라이선스 계약 체결, Mellanox처럼 NVIDIA 아키텍처에 통합 예정
BofA Q: 클라우드 캐팩스 $700B 지속 가능성 및 CY2027 성장 가시성 문의
젠슨 황: NVLink 72 에너지효율 이전 세대 대비 50배 향상, 토큰 생성이 미래 소프트웨어의 핵심
클래식 컴퓨팅 대비 AI 연산 수요 1,000배, 2030년 데이터센터 캐팩스 3~4조 달러 전망 유효
모든 기업이 AI 팩토리 통해 토큰 생산 — 클라우드·엔터프라이즈·로보틱스 전 산업 포괄
UBS Q: 자본 배분 전략 — 구매 약정 급증 대비 자사주 매입 확대 여지 문의
CFO: 공급망·생태계 지원 최우선, 자사주 매입·배당 지속 중
골드만삭스 Q: 2030 데이터센터 캐팩스 3~4조 달러 목표 유효성 및 핵심 동인 문의
어젠틱 AI 2~3개월 전 변곡점 돌파 — NVIDIA 내 Claude Code·Codex·Cursor 풀 활용
Anthropic 매출 1년 10배 성장에도 공급 부족 — 토큰 수요 기하급수적 증가 방증
우주 AI: Hopper GPU 우주 배치 완료, 초고해상도 위성 이미징 AI 처리 핵심 활용

key_points: 어젠틱 AI 변곡점 선언 — 2~3개월 전 개막, 코딩·문제해결 실용화 단계 진입
NVLink 72: 이전 세대 대비 에너지효율 토큰 생성 50배 향상
2030년 데이터센터 캐팩스 3~4조 달러 전망 유효 — AI 소프트웨어가 클래식 대비 1,000배 연산 필요
Groq와 저지연 추론 기술 비독점 라이선스 계약 체결
Hopper GPU 우주 배치 완료 — 세계 최초 우주 GPU
Anthropic 매출 1년 10배 성장에도 공급 부족 — NVIDIA 수요 급증 방증
CY2027 성장 가시성 확보 (대규모 구매 약정 반영)
speech_summary.full_summary: NVIDIA Q4 FY26 실적발표 어닝콜. 젠슨 황 CEO는 어젠틱 AI가 2~3개월 전 변곡점을 돌파했다고 선언하며, 코딩·문제해결 분야에서 이미 실용화 단계에 접어들었음을 강조했다. NVLink 72 아키텍처(Blackwell)로 이전 세대 대비 에너지 효율 50배 향상을 달성했으며, AI 소프트웨어가 요구하는 연산량은 클래식 컴퓨팅 대비 1,000배에 달해 2030년 데이터센터 캐팩스 3~4조 달러 전망이 충분히 합리적이라고 주장했다. Anthropic·Meta·OpenAI·xAI 등 주요 AI 기업 전방위 파트너십을 발표했으며, 저지연 추론 기업 Groq와의 비독점 라이선스 계약을 체결해 NVIDIA 아키텍처에 통합 예정이라고 밝혔다. Hopper GPU가 이미 우주에 배치되어 초고해상도 위성 이미징 AI 처리에 활용 중임도 공개했다. CFO 콜레트 크레스는 공급망 생태계 지원을 최우선으로 하면서도 자사주 매입·배당을 지속 중이라고 밝혔다.
speech_summary.market_impact_summary: 어닝콜 종료(23:07 UTC, 08:07 KST) 직후 GOOGL·AMD·MU·AVGO 애프터마켓에서 강한 변동성 포착. 특히 GOOGL(23:14 UTC, 1위)과 AMD(23:15 UTC, 2위)가 어닝콜 종료 직후 즉각 반응. MU는 어닝콜 도중(22:57~23:05 UTC) 이미 반응하며 젠슨 황 AI 발언 구간과 시간적 연동. BTC·ETH도 리스크온 분위기 동반 반응. NQ/ES 선물 데이터는 38일 경과로 수집 불가.

[clears throat] Hi, thanks a lot for those my question. Um, I'd like to ask about space data centers uh which some of your customers are considering. How feasible do you think that is and what kind of horizon? Um, and what do the economics look like today and how do you think that could evolve over time? Thank you. Well, the economics are poor today, but it's going to improve over time. Uh, as you know, as you know, the way that space works is radically different than how it works down here. There's an abundance of energy. Um, uh, but solar panels are large, but there's plenty of space in space. Uh, the en the heat dissipation, it's cold in space. Um, however, uh, there's no there's no air flow. And so the only way to to uh dissipate heat is through conduction and the radiators that you need to create are fairly large. Um liquid cooling is obviously out of the question because it's kind of it's heavy and you know freezes and and so the methods that we use here on Earth are a little different than the way we would do it in space. Um but there are many different computing problems that really wants to be done in space. And so uh Nvidia is is already the world's first GPU in space. Hopper is in space. And um uh one of the best use cases of uh GPUs in space is imaging to be able to imaging image at extremely high resolutions using using of course of course uh optics and and and artificial intelligence and to be able to do that computation of reprojection of different angles and be able to to upres and ando noise reduction and and just be able to see be able to image at very large, very high resolutions, extremely large scales and very, very fast. It's hard to do that um by sending, you know, pabytes and pedabytes of imaging data back here on Earth and doing that work. It's easier just to do it out in space and then and then um ignore ignore all of the data collected and processed until you see something interesting. And so artificial intelligence in space will

And so the amount of token generation capability uh that the world needs is a lot more than $700 billion. And and uh I'm fairly confidenthat we're going to continue to generate tokens. We're going to continue to invest in compute capacity um from this point out and and uh fundamentally becausevery single company depends on software. Every software will depend on AI and so every company will produce tokens and that's the reason why I call them AI factories and and uh whether your company in the cloud data centers you have AI factories to generate tokens for your revenues. If you're enterprise software company, you're going to generate tokens for the agentic systems that are on top of your tools. Uh if you are a robotics factory um and uh self-driving cars first indication of that, you have huge supercomputers which are basically AI factories to generate tokens um that goes into your cars that becomes its AI and then you also have to put computers inside the cars to continuously generate tokens. Um but now everything is generative in real time and when it's generated in real time it could take into context of the person the situation um the query and the intentions could all be taken into into consideration to generate the outcome of this new software called we call AI agentic AI and so the amount of computationecessary um is far far greater than pre-recorded You know, just as just as a a computer has a lot more computation capability than a DVD recorder, a DVD player that was pre-recorded, artificial intelligence needs a lot more computing capability than the way we used to do software in the past.
